action.reasoning: Observation: The previous iteration failed because torchrun could not resolve the container hostname for distributed rendezvous (TCPStore timeout). Analysis: We need a more robust multi-GPU launcher that doesn't rely on torchrun's automatic hostname detection. Decision: Switch to torch.multiprocessing.spawn inside train.py, explicitly setting MASTER_ADDR=127.0.0.1. To avoid fork-after-CUDA deadlocks in DataLoader workers, I'll set num_workers=0 for this baseline run. I'll also make predict.py more robust to checkpoint format. Then train 2 epochs, locally test predict.py on a tiny dummy split, and submit eval to measure iteration time and establish a baseline worst_region_accuracy.
action.command: OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 32 --lr 1e-5 --weight_decay 0.1 --label_smoothing 0.1 --num_workers 0 --output_dir checkpoints && mkdir -p dummy_test && head -n 20 /data/val.txt | awk '{print $1}' > dummy_test/test.txt && ln -sf /data/images dummy_test/images && python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output dummy_test/out.json && echo "PREDICT_OK"
